step_indices_raw.shape[0]\n113\t    if total_requests == 0:\n114\t        return\n115\t\n116\t    if dst.device != src.device:\n117\t        raise ValueError(\n118\t            f\"dst and src must be on the same device. {dst.device=} {src.device=}\"\n119\t        )\n120\t    if not dst.is_cuda or not src.is_cuda:\n121\t        raise ValueError(\n122\t            \"fused_mamba_state_scatter_with_mask only supports CUDA tensors.\"\n123\t        )\n124\t    if dst.ndim < 2 or src.ndim < 3:\n125\t        raise ValueError(f\"Unexpected tensor ranks: {dst.ndim=} # Get strides (in elements, not bytes)\n152\t    src_layer_stride = src.stride(0)\n153\t    src_req_stride = src.stride(1)\n154\t    src_step_stride = src.stride(2)\n155\t    dst_layer_stride = dst.stride(0)\n156\t    dst_req_stride = dst.stride(1)\n157\t\n158\t    # Ensure indices are int32 and contiguous\n159\t    dst_indices_raw = dst_indices_raw.to(torch.int32).contiguous()\n160\t    step_indices_raw = step_indices_raw.to(torch.int32).contiguous()\n161\t\n162\t    # Ensure tensors are contiguous\n163\t    if not dst.is_contiguous():\n164\t        raise ValueError(\"dst tensor must        **kwargs,\n599\t    ):\n600\t        buffers = self.buffers\n601\t        num_tokens = len(forward_batch.input_ids)\n602\t        index = bisect.bisect_left(self.capture_num_tokens, num_tokens)\n603\t        static_num_tokens = self.capture_num_tokens[index]\n604\t        self.raw_num_tokens = num_tokens\n605\t        if static_num_tokens != num_tokens:\n606\t            buffers.out_cache_loc.zero_()\n607\t            if buffers.out_cache_loc_swa is not None:\n608\t                buffers.out_cache_loc_swa.zero_()\n609\t            buffers.input_ids[num_tokens:static_num_tokens].zero_()\n610\t            buffers.positions[num_tokens:static_num_tokens].zero_()\n611\t            if self.is_multimodal:\n612\t                buffers.input_embeds[:, num_tokens:static_num_tokens].zero_()\n613\t            if forward_batch.mrope_positions is not None:\n614\t                buffers.mrope_positions[:, num_tokens:static_num_tokens].zero_()\n615\t\n616\t        bs = forward_batch.batch_size\n617\t\n618\t        buffers.input_ids[:num_tokens].copy_(forward_batch.input_ids)\n619\t        buffers.positions[:num_tokens].copy_(forward_batch.positions)\n620\t        buffers.out_cache_loc[:num_tokens].copy_(forward_batch.out_cache_loc)\n621\t        if buffers.out_cache_loc_swa is not     forward_batch.out_cache_loc\n625\t                )\n626\t            )\n627\t\n628\t        if (\n629\t            buffers.mamba_track_indices is not None\n630\t            and \"\"\"Call torch._foreach_copy_ grouped by (dst_dtype, src_dtype) pairs.\"\"\"\n101\t\n102\t    def foreach_copy(dsts: List[torch.Tensor], srcs: List[torch.Tensor]) -> None:\n103\t        if seq_len_fill_value,\n229\t            dtype=torch.int32,\n230\t            device=\"cpu\",\n231\t        )\n232\t\n233\t        return cls(\n234\t            input_ids=input_ids,\n235\t            input_embeds=input_embeds,\n236\t            req_pool_indices=req_pool_indices,\n237\t            seq_lens=seq_lens,\n238\t            seq_lens_cpu=seq_lens_cpu,\n239\t            out_cache_loc=out_cache_loc,\n240\t            positions=positions,\n241\t            mrope_positions=mrope_positions,\n242\t            num_token_non_padded=num_token_non_padded,\n243\t            custom_mask=custom_mask,\n244\t            next_token_logits_buffer=next_token_logits_buffer,\n245\t            mamba_track_indices=mamba_track_indices,\n246\t            mamba_track_mask=mamba_track_mask,\n247\t            encoder_lens=encoder_lens,\n248\t            global_num_tokens_gpu=global_num_tokens_gpu,\n249\t )\n253\t\n254\t    def populate_from_forward_batch(\n255\t        self,\n256\t        *,\n257\t        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,\n258\t        raw_bs: int,\n259\t        raw_num_token: int,\n260\t        bs: int,\n261\t        seq_len_fill_value: int,\n262\t        require_gathered_buffer: bool,\n263\t        num_tokens_per_bs: int,\n264\t        nsa_enable_prefill_cp: bool,\n265\t        enable_num_token_non_padded_flag: bool,\n266\t        pp_proxy_tensors: Optional[PPProxyTensors] = None,\n267\t    ):\n268\t        if bs != raw_bs:\n269\t            self.seq_lens.fill_(seq_len_fill_value)\n270\t            self.out_cache_loc.zero_()\n271\t            if self.mamba_track_indices is not None:\n272\t                self.mamba_track_indices.zero_()\n273\t            if self.mamba_track_mask is not None:\n274\t                self.mamba_track_mask.fill_(False)\n275\t\n276\t        # Build batched copy lists for all GPU tensors.\n277\t        dsts = [\n278\t            self.input_ids[:raw_num_token],\n279\t            self.req_pool_indices[:raw_bs],\n280\t            self.seq_lens[:raw_bs],\n281\t            self.out_cache_loc[:raw_num_token],\n282\t            self.positions[:raw_num_token],\n283\t        ]\n284\t        srcs = [\n285\t            forward_batch.input_ids,\n286\t            forward_batch.req_pool_indices,\n287\t            forward_batch.seq_lens,\n288\t            forward_batch.out_cache_loc,\n289\t            forward_batch.positions,\n290\t        ]\n291\t\n292\t        if self.ngram_embedding_info is not None:\n293\t            ngram_embedding_info = forward_batch.ngram_embedding_info\n294\t            self.ngram_embedding_info.column_starts[:raw_bs].copy_(\n295\t                ngram_embedding_info.column_starts\n296\t            )\n297\t            self.ngram_embedding_info.req_lens[:raw_bs].copy_(\n298\t                ngram_embedding_info.req_lens\n299\t            )\n300\t\n301\t        if (\n302\t            self.mamba_track_indices is not None\n303\t            and forward_batch.mamba_track_indices is not None\n304\t        ):\n305\t            dsts.append(self.mamba_track_indices[:raw_bs])\n306\t            srcs.append(forward_batch.mamba_track_indices)\n307\t        if (\n308\t            self.mamba_track_mask is not None\n309\t            and forward_batch.mamba_track_mask is not None\n310\t        ):\n311\t            dsts.append(self.mamba_track_mask[:raw_bs])\n312\t            srcs.append(forward_batch.mamba_track_mask)\n313\t\n314\t        if self.encoder_lens is not None and forward_batch.encoder_lens is not None:\n315\t            dsts.append(self.encoder_lens[:raw_bs])\n316\t            srcs.append(forward_batch.encoder_lens)\n317\t\n318\t        if forward_batch.mrope_positions is not None:\n319\t            dsts.append(self.mrope_positions[:, :raw_num_token])\n320\t            srcs.append(forward_batch.mrope_positions)\n321\t\n322\t        if require_gathered_buffer:\n323\t            self.global_num_tokens_gpu.fill_(bs * num_tokens_per_bs)\n324\t            self.global_num_tokens_for_logprob_gpu.fill_(bs * num_tokens_per_bs)\n325\t\n326\t        if enable_num_token_non_padded_flag:\n327\t            if require_gathered_buffer and not nsa_enable_prefill_cp:\n328\t                num_tokens_per_dp = bs * num_tokens_per_bs\n329\t                local = compute_local_num_token_non_padded(\n330\t                    global_num_token_non_padded=forward_batch.num_token_non_padded,\n331\t                    num_tokens_per_dp=num_tokens_per_dp,\n332\t                )\n333\t                dsts.append(self.num_token_non_padded)\n334\t                srcs.append(local)\n335\t            else:\n336\t                dsts.append(self.num_token_non_padded)\n337\t                srcs.append(forward_batch.num_token_non_padded)\n338\t\n339\t        # Pipeline-parallel proxy tensors.\n340\t        if pp_proxy_tensors is not None and self.pp_proxy_tensors is not None:\n341\t            for key, buf in self.pp_proxy_tensors.items():\n342\t                src = pp_proxy_tensors.tensors[key]\n343\t                dim = src.shape[0]\n344\t                dsts.append(buf[:dim])\n345\t                srcs.append(src)\n346\t\n347\t        # Batch all GPU copies, grouped by dtype pair.\n348\t        _grouped_foreach_copy_(dsts, srcs)\n349\t\n350\t        # CPU tensor copy (cannot be batched with GPU tensors).\n351\t        if forward_batch.seq_lens_cpu is not None:\n352\t            if bs != raw_bs:\n353\t                self.seq_lens_cpu.fill_(seq_len_fill_value)\n354\t            self.seq_lens_cpu[:raw_bs].copy_(forward_batch.seq_lens_cpu)\n355\t\n356\t\n357\t# Detect whether the current forward pass is in capture mode\n358\tis_capture_mode = False\n359\t\n360\t\n361\tdef get_is_capture_mode():\n362\t    return is_capture_mode\n363\t\n364\t\n365\t@contextmanager\n366\tdef model_capture_mode():\n367\t    global is_capture_mode\n368\t    is_capture_mode = True\n369\t\n370\t    yield\n371\t\n372\t    is_capture_mode = False\n373\t\n374\t\n375\t@contextmanager\n376\tdef freeze_gc(enable_cudagraph_gc: bool):\n377\t    \"\"\"\n378\t    Optimize garbage collection during CUDA graph capture.\n379\t    Clean up, then freeze all remaining objects from being included\n380\t    in future collections if GC is disabled during capture.\n381\t    \"\"\"\n382\t    gc.collect()\n383\t    should_freeze = not enable_cudagraph_gc\n384\t    if should_freeze:\n385\t        gc.freeze()\n386\t    try:\n387\t        yield\n388\t    finally:\n389\t        if should_freeze:\n390\t            gc.unfreeze()\n391\t            gc.collect()\n392\t\n393\t\n394\tdef _to_torch(model: torch.nn.Module, reverse: bool, num_tokens: int):\n395\t    for sub in model._modules.values():\n396\t        if isinstance(sub, MultiPlatformOp):\n397\t            if reverse:\n398\t                sub.leave_torch_compile()\n399\t            else:\n400\t                sub.enter_torch_compile(num_tokens=num_tokens)\n401\t        if isinstance(sub, torch.nn.Module):\n402\t            _to_torch(sub, reverse, num_tokens)\n403\t\n404\t\n405\t@contextmanager\n406\tdef patch_model(\n407\t    model: torch.nn.Module,\n408\t    enable_compile: bool,\n409\t    num_tokens: int,\n410\t    tp_group: GroupCoordinator,\n411\t):\n412\t    \"\"\"Patch the model to make it compatible with with torch.compile\"\"\"\n413\t    backup_ca_comm = None\n414\t\n415\t    try:\n416\t        if enable_compile:\n417\t            _to_torch(model, reverse=False, num_tokens=num_tokens)\n418\t            backup_ca_comm = tp_group.ca_comm\n419\t            # Use custom-allreduce here.\n420\t            # We found the custom allreduce is much faster than the built-in allreduce in torch,\n421\t            # even with ENABLE_INTRA_NODE_COMM=1.\n422\t            # tp_group.ca_comm = None\n423\t            yield torch.compile(\n424\t                torch.no_grad()(model.forward),\n425\t                mode=os.environ.get(\n426\t                    \"SGLANG_TORCH_COMPILE_MODE\", \"max-autotune-no-cudagraphs\"\n427\t                ),\n428\t                dynamic=_is_hip and get_bool_env_var(\"SGLANG_TORCH_DYNAMIC_SHAPE\"),\n429\t            )\n430\t        else:\n431\t            yield model.forward\n432\t    finally:\n433\t        if enable_compile:\n434\t            _to_torch(model, reverse=True, num_tokens=num_tokens)\n435\t            tp_group.ca_comm = backup_ca_comm\n436\t\n437\t\n438\tdef set_torch_compile_config():\n439\t    import torch._dynamo.config\n440\t    import torch._inductor.config\n441\t\n442\t    torch._inductor.config.coordinate_descent_tuning = True\n443\t    torch._inductor.config.triton.unique_kernel_names = True\n444\t    torch._inductor.config.fx_graph_cache = True  # Experimental feature to reduce compilation times, will be on by default in future\n445\t\n446\t    # FIXME: tmp workaround\n447\t    torch._dynamo.config.accumulated_cache_size_limit = 1024\n448\t    if hasattr(torch._dynamo.config, \"cache_size_limit\"):\n449\t        torch._dynamo.config.cache_size_limit = 1024\n450\t\n451\t    monkey_patch_torch_compile()\n452\t\n453\t\n454\tdef get_batch_sizes_to_capture(model_runner: ModelRunner, num_tokens_per_bs=1):\n455\t    server_args = model_runner.server_args\n456\t    capture_bs = server_args.cuda_graph_bs\n457\t    num_max_requests = model_runner.req_to_token_pool.size\n458\t\n459\t    mul_base = 1\n460\t    if server_args.enable_two_batch_overlap:\n461\t        mul_base *= 2\n462\t        num_tokens_per_bs = 1  # tbo not test, set num_tokens_per_bs to 1\n463\t\n464\t    if require_gathered_buffer(server_args):\n465\t        mul_base *= get_attention_tp_size()\n466\t\n467\t    if mul_base % get_attention_cp_size() != 0:\n468\t        mul_base *= get_attention_cp_size()\n469\t\n470\t    # pad `num_max_requests` to avoid being filtered out\n471\t    num_max_requests = (num_max_requests + mul_base - 1) // mul_base * mul_base\n472\t    if max(capture_bs) > num_max_requests:\n473\t        # In some cases (e.g., with a small GPU or --max-running-requests), the #max-running-requests\n474\t        # is very small. We add more values here to make sure we capture the maximum bs.\n475\t        capture_bs += [num_max_requests]\n476\t\n477\t    # Model input token count = bs * num_tokens_per_bs; must be a multiple of attn_tp_size.\n478\t    capture_bs = [bs for bs in capture_bs if bs * num_tokens_per_bs % mul_base == 0]\n479\t    capture_bs = [bs for bs in capture_bs if bs <= num_max_requests]\n480\t    capture_bs = list(sorted(set(capture_bs)))\n481\t\n482\t    assert len(capture_bs) > 0 and capture_bs[0] > 0, f\"{capture_bs=}\"\n483\t    compile_bs = (\n484\t        [bs for bs in capture_bs if bs <= server_args.torch_compile_max_bs]\n485\t        if server_args.enable_torch_compile\n486\t        else []\n487\t    )\n488\t    return capture_bs, compile_bs\n489\t\n490\t\n491\t# Reuse this memory pool across all cuda graph runners.\n492\tglobal_graph_memory_pool = None\n493\t\n494\t\n495\tdef get_global_graph_memory_pool():\n496\t    return global_graph_memory_pool\n497\t\n498\t\n499\tdef set_global_graph_memory_pool(val):\n500\t    global global_graph_memory_pool\n501\t    global_graph_memory_pool = val\n502\t\n503\t\n504\tclass CudaGraphRunner:\n505\t    \"\"\"A CudaGraphRunner runs        )\n599\t\n600\t        if self.enable_torch_compile:\n601\t            set_torch_compile_config()\n602\t\n603\t        if self.model_runner.server_args.enable_lora:\n604\t            # Phase 2 self.require_mlp_tp_gather:\n670\t            cuda_graph_bs = (\n671\t                max(forward_batch.global_num_tokens_cpu) // self.num_tokens_per_bs\n672\t                if    )\n676\t        else:\n677\t            cuda_graph_bs = forward_batch.batch_size\n678\t\n679\t        graph_key = cuda_graph_bs\n680\t        if self.enable_pdmux:\n681\t            graph_key =     # NOTE: cuda graph cannot handle mixed batch (encoder_len = 0)\n693\t        # If mixed batch cannot be supported, then encoder_lens can be removed in cuda graph\n694\t        # because the (\n704\t                forward_batch.spec_info.capture_hidden_mode\n705\t                if getattr(forward_batch.spec_info, \"capture_hidden_mode\", None)\n706\t                is not None\n707\t                else CaptureHiddenMode.NULL\n708\t            ),\n709\t        )\n710\t        capture_hidden_mode_matches = (\n711\t            requested_capture_hidden_mode == self.enable_two_batch_overlap else True\n716\t        )\n717\t\n718\t        is_ngram_supported = (\n719\t            (\n720\t                forward_batch.batch_size * self.num_tokens_per_bs\n721\t                == forward_batch.input_ids.numel()\n722\t            )\n723\t            if self.model_runner.spec_algorithm.is_ngram()\n724\t            else True\n725\t        )\n726\t\n727\t and is_ngram_supported\n733\t        )\n734\t\n735\t    def _init_profile_context_and_memory_record(self):\n736\t        profile_context = profile(\n737\t            activities=[ProfilerActivity.CPU, self.model_runner.gpu_id,\n768\t                empty_cache=False,\n769\t            )\n770\t            # Reverse the order to enable better memory sharing across cuda graphs.\n771\t empty_cache=False,\n782\t                    )\n783\t                    capture_range.set_description(\n784\t                        f\"Capturing batches ({bs=} {avail_mem=:.2f} GB)\"\n785\t _capture_one_stream()\n810\t            else:\n811\t                set_pdmux_status(False)\n812\t                for i, sg in enumerate(self.stream_groups):\n813\t                    with spec_info\n1228\t\n1229\t\n1230\tCUDA_GRAPH_CAPTURE_FAILED_MSG = (\n1231\t    \"Possible solutions:\\n\"\n1232\t    \"1. set --mem-fraction-static to a smaller value (e.g., 0.8 or 0.7)\\n\"\n1233\t `cuda_graph_runner.py`.\n\n---\n\n## Summary of How CUDA Graph Capture/Replay Handles Mamba State\n\n### Capture Phase\n\nBoth runners allocate static GPU buffers for mamba tracking tensors during initialization:\n- `mamba_track_indices` (int64) -- maps batch slots to mamba state indices\n- `mamba_track_mask` (bool) -- indicates which batch slots have valid mamba state\n- `mamba_track_seqlens` (int32) -- only in `PiecewiseCudaGraphRunner` (prefill path); tracks sequence lengths for mamba state\n\nThese static buffers are sliced to the batch size being captured and passed into the `ForwardBatch` that is used during CUDA graph recording. The actual mamba state management (conv state, SSM state) is not directly handled by these buffers -- they only carry tracking/indexing metadata. The mamba layers themselves manage state through the `req_to_token_pool` and mamba-specific state pools, which are referenced indirectly through these indices.\n\n### Replay Phase\n\nDuring replay, the runners copy the live `forward_batch` mamba tracking tensors into the pre-allocated static buffers via `.copy_()`. In `CudaGraphRunner` (decode path), this is done through the batched `_grouped_foreach_copy_` mechanism (lines 302-312). In `PiecewiseCudaGraphRunner` (prefill/extend path), individual `.copy_()` calls are used (lines 628-642). The padded region of `mamba_track_indices` is zeroed and `mamba_track_mask` is filled with `False` when padding is needed (lines 271-274 in `CudaGraphRunner`), ensuring the model does not process invalid mamba state for padded batch slots.\n\n### Key Difference Between the Two Runners\n\n`CudaGraphRunner` sets `mamba_track_seqlens=None` (line 950, commented \"Prefill only\") because it handles decode. `PiecewiseCudaGraphRunner` fully supports `mamba_track_seqlens` because it handles extend/prefill where sequence length information is needed for mamba state initialization.\n\n### The `.item()` Synchronization Point\n\nThe single `.item()` call at line 947 of `cuda_graph_runner.py` (`seq_lens.sum().item()`) occurs only during capture, not during replay, so it does not cause runtime synchronization during inference. The `PiecewiseCudaGraphRunner` avoids `.item()` entirely by passing integer values directly (e.g., `seq_lens_sum=num_tokens` at line 524)."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11735,"cache_creation_input_tokens":19754,"cache_read_input_tokens":8618,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":19754,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01U2TE1vZR75FnKgfDaThveW","session_id":"e1d24818-237a-4c5f-af40-26ac59345739","uuid":"f8d0b655-c672-47a5-b1a5-3fd13a458754","timestamp":"2026-07-17T16:30:03.546Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011Cd7w2cGZeUNPBdEBx8aQd","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Search cuda graph runner mamba"}

static_forward_batch construction with mamba (lines 713-715):**\n```python\nmamba_track_indices=mamba_track_indices,\nmamba_track_mask=mamba_track_mask,\nmamba_track_seqlens=mamba_track_seqlens,\n```\n\n### `.item()` Calls\n\nNone found in this file.\n\n### `.cpu()` Calls\n\nNone found in this file (CPU tensors are created directly with `device=\"cpu\"`).\n\n### `.tolist()` Calls\n\nNone found in this file.\n\n### `torch.cuda.synchronize()` / `device_module.synchronize()` Calls\n\n**Line 314** (inside `__init__`, after compilation warmup):\n```python\nself.device_module.synchronize()\n```\n\n**Lines 588-590** (inside `capture_one_batch_size`):\n```python\nfor _ in range(2):\n    self.device_module.synchronize()\n    self.model_runner.tp_group.barrier()\n    run_once()\n```\nSame warmup pattern as in `cuda_graph_runner.py`.\n\n---\n\n## Summary of How CUDA Graph Capture/Replay Handles Mamba State\n\n### Capture Phase\n\nBoth runners allocate static GPU buffers for mamba tracking tensors during initialization:\n- `mamba_track_indices` (int64) -- maps batch slots to mamba state indices\n- `mamba_track_mask` (bool) -- indicates which batch slots have valid mamba state\n- `mamba_track_seqlens` (int32) -- only in `PiecewiseCudaGraphRunner` (prefill path); tracks sequence lengths for mamba state\n\nThese static buffers are sliced to the batch size being captured and passed into the `ForwardBatch` that is used during CUDA graph recording. The actual mamba state management (conv state, SSM state) is not directly handled by these buffers -- they only carry tracking/indexing metadata. The mamba layers themselves manage state through the `req_to_token_pool` and mamba-specific state pools, which are referenced indirectly through these indices.\n\n### Replay Phase\n\nDuring replay, the runners copy the live `forward_batch` mamba tracking tensors into the pre-allocated static buffers via `.copy_()`. In `CudaGraphRunner` (decode path), this is done through the batched `_grouped_foreach_copy_` mechanism (lines 302-312). In `PiecewiseCudaGraphRunner` (prefill/extend path), individual `.copy_()` calls are used (lines 628-642). The padded region of `mamba_track_indices` is zeroed and `mamba_track_mask` is filled with `False` when padding is needed (lines 271-274 in `CudaGraphRunner`), ensuring the model does not process invalid mamba state for padded batch slots.\n\n### Key Difference Between the Two Runners\n\n`CudaGraphRunner` sets `mamba_track_seqlens=None` (line 950, commented \"Prefill only\") because it handles decode. `PiecewiseCudaGraphRunner` fully supports `mamba_track_seqlens` because it handles extend/prefill where sequence length information is needed for mamba state initialization.\n\n### The `.item()` Synchronization Point\n\nThe single `.item()` call at line 947 of `cuda_graph_runner.py` (`seq_lens.sum().item()`) occurs only during capture, not during replay, so it does not cause runtime synchronization during inference. The `PiecewiseCudaGraphRunner` avoids `.item()` entirely by passing integer values directly (e.g., `seq_lens_sum=num_tokens` at line 524).","usage":{"total_tokens":40111,"tool_uses":2,"duration_ms":56384},"uuid":"656d4f51-7bdc-462b-9066-31cbed7ea851","session_id":"e1d24818-237a-4c5f-af40-26ac59345739"}

We only track the conv state up to the last complete chunk boundary\n248\t        (aligned_len).\n249\t\n250\t        start_indices is the starting token index of the conv state to track in this extend batch.\n251\t        indices include all pos to track in this extend batch, conv_state_len for each req that\n252\t        needs to be tracked (i.e. mamba_track_mask is True)\n253\t\n254\t        Returns:\n255\t            indices: Tensor of shape [num_tracked_requests, conv_state_len] containing\n256\t                     flattened positions into the packed input tensor.\n257\t        \"\"\"\n258\t        conv_state_len = self.conv_states_shape[-1]\n259\t\n260\t        # Calculate the end position of the last aligned chunk\n261\t        lens_to_track = (\n262\t            forward_batch.mamba_track_seqlens - forward_batch.extend_prefix_lens\n263\t        )\n264\t        mamba_cache_chunk_size = get_global_server_args().mamba_cache_chunk_size\n265\t        aligned_len = (lens_to_track // mamba_cache_chunk_size) * mamba_cache_chunk_size\n266\t        start_indices = query_start_loc[:-1] + aligned_len - conv_state_len\n267\t        start_indices = start_indices[forward_batch.mamba_track_mask]\n268\t\n269\t        # Create indices: [batch_size, conv_state_len]\n270\t        indices = start_indices.unsqueeze(-1) + torch.arange(\n271\t            conv_state_len,\n272\t            device=self.device,\n273\t            dtype=start_indices.dtype,\n274\t        )\n275\t\n276\t        return indices.clamp(0, query_start_loc[-1] - Here are the five occurrences:\n\n**Lines 549-550** (`cache_finished_req`, paged path):\n```python\n# Lines 544-555\n                token_ids = token_ids[:cache_len]\n                kv_indices = kv_indices[:cache_len]\n\n            if self.page_size != 1:\n                page_aligned_len = len(kv_indices) // self.page_size * self.page_size\n                page_aligned_kv_indices = kv_indices[:page_aligned_len].to(\n                    dtype=torch.int64, copy=True\n                )\n            else:\n                page_aligned_len = len(kv_indices)\n                page_aligned_kv_indices = kv_indices.to(dtype=torch.int64, copy=True)\n```\n\n**Line 554** (`cache_finished_req`, non-paged path):\nSame block as above, the `else` branch.\n\n**Line 614** (`cache_unfinished_req`, skip path):\n```python\n# Lines 609-615\n            kv_indices = self.req_to_token_pool.req_to_token[\n                req.req_pool_idx, : len(req.fill_ids)\n            ]\n\n            # `req.prefix_indices` will be used in `PrefillAdder::add_chunked_req` later\n            req.prefix_indices = kv_indices.to(dtype=torch.int64, copy=True)\n            return\n```\n\n**Lines 633-634** (`cache_unfinished_req`, paged path):\n```python\n# Lines 628-639\n        kv_indices = kv_indices_orig[:cache_len]\n        if self.page_size != 1:\n            page_aligned_len = len(kv_indices) // self.page_size * self.page_size\n            page_aligned_kv_indices = kv_indices[:page_aligned_len].to(\n                dtype=torch.int64, copy=True\n            )\n        else:\n            page_aligned_len = len(kv_indices)\n            page_aligned_kv_indices = kv_indices.to(dtype=torch.int64, copy=True)\n```\n\n**Line 638** (`cache_unfinished_req`, non-paged path):\nSame block as above, the `else` branch.\n\n---\n\n## 2. How `extra_buffer` (specifically `enable_mamba_extra_buffer`) Is Used\n\nThe flag `self.enable_mamba_extra_buffer` is set from `params.enable_mamba_extra_buffer` at **line 433** (defaults to `False` per `CacheInitParams` at `cache_init_params.py:28`).\n\n### Constraint when disabled (line 435-438):\nWhen `enable_mamba_extra_buffer` is `False`, page_size must be 1. This is the \"v1\" mode.\n\n### Effect on `cache_finished_req` (lines 533-601):\n\n**Determining `cache_len`** (lines 535-538): When enabled, `cache_len = req.mamba_last_track_seqlen` (the sequence length at the last mamba state checkpoint). When disabled, `cache_len = len(token_ids)` (full sequence).\n\n**Constructing `mamba_value`** (lines 562-577): When enabled, the mamba value is taken from `req.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer` -- it reads from the \"other\" ping-pong buffer (the one NOT being written to next, i.e., the one that has the last committed state), unsqueezes and clones. When disabled, the mamba value comes directly from `req.mamba_pool_idx`.\n\n**Free logic** (line 595): When enabled, `free_mamba_cache` is always `True` (the request's mamba cache is always freed since the state was captured into the radix tree). When disabled, free only happens if `mamba_exist` is `True` (i.e., the tree already had a mamba state at that node).\n\n**Passing `mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer_to_keep`** (lines 598-601): When enabled, this buffer index is passed to `free_mamba_cache` so the pool can selectively keep one ping-pong buffer. When disabled, it is `None`.\n\n### Effect on `cache_unfinished_req` (lines 618-657):\n\nSame pattern: `cache_len` is `req.mamba_last_track_seqlen` (enabled) vs `len(token_ids)` (disabled), and `mamba_value` is sourced from the ping-pong track buffer (enabled) vs direct mamba pool indices (disabled).\n\n### Summary of extra_buffer purpose:\nThe \"extra buffer\" mechanism implements a **ping-pong double-buffering** scheme for mamba states. During forward passes, the model writes mamba states into alternating buffers. When caching a request (finished or unfinished), the system reads from the buffer that was last fully written (the \"other\" one relative to `mamba_next_track_idx`). This allows mamba state snapshots to be taken at chunk boundaries (`mamba_last_track_seqlen`) rather than requiring the full sequence to be processed, enabling chunked prefill for Mamba/hybrid models. When disabled (v1 mode), there is a single mamba pool index per request and the entire sequence must be processed.\n\n---\n\n## 3. How Mamba State Tracking Interacts with Prefix Caching\n\n### Radix tree node structure (lines 66-107):\nEach `TreeNode` has:\n- `value`: KV cache indices (full attention)\n- `mamba_value`: Mamba state pool indices\n- `mamba_host_value`: Mamba state backed up to host\n- Separate lock reference counts: `full_lock_ref` and `mamba_lock_ref`\n\n### \"Tombstone\" concept:\nA **mamba tombstone** is a tree node where `mamba_value is None` but the node still exists with KV cache data (`value`). This arises because mamba states can be evicted independently from full KV cache. The property `mamba_evicted` (line 114-115) checks this.\n\n### Dual LRU lists (lines 462-473):\nThe cache maintains **two separate LRU lists**: `full_lru_list` for KV cache eviction order, and `mamba_lru_list` for mamba state eviction order. The mamba LRU list only contains nodes with non-None `mamba_value` (non-tombstoned nodes).\n\n### Lock reference invariant (lines 79-83):\n- If `mamba_lock_ref > 0`, then `full_lock_ref > 0` (mamba lock implies full lock)\n- If `full_lock_ref > 0`, `mamba_lock_ref` can be 0 (full lock does NOT imply mamba lock)\n- Full lock propagates from node to root; mamba lock only applies to the node itself\n\n### Prefix matching with mamba awareness (lines 956-996, `_match_prefix_helper`):\nThe prefix matching algorithm tracks `best_value_len` and `best_last_node`. It only updates these when it encounters a node with `mamba_value is not None`. This means the effective prefix match stops at the **deepest non-tombstoned node**. If intermediate nodes are tombstoned (mamba evicted), the prefix match truncates to the last node that has a mamba state, because the mamba state is needed to continue computation.\n\n### Post-processing with mamba branching (lines 1007-1079, `_match_post_processor`):\nWhen the actual matched value extends beyond `best_value_len` (meaning there are tombstoned nodes deeper in the tree), the system computes `mamba_branching_seqlen` -- a chunk-aligned sequence length indicating where the mamba state fork should happen. This tells the model runner that tokens beyond this point need re-computation of mamba states even though KV cache exists.\n\n### Copy-on-write mamba states (lines 1049-1066):\nDuring prefix matching with `cow_mamba=True`, the matched node's mamba state is **copied** into the request's private mamba pool. This is copy-on-write: the tree's mamba state is not modified; instead, a fresh copy is allocated for the request to use during its forward pass. If allocation fails, the system evicts mamba states (while protecting the matched node) and retries.\n\n### Eviction: independent mamba eviction (lines 775-808, `evict_mamba`):\nMamba states can be evicted independently from KV cache. When an **internal node** (has children) is mamba-evicted, it becomes a tombstone -- its `mamba_value` is set to `None`, it's removed from the mamba LRU list, but its KV cache data (`value`) and position in the full LRU list remain. When a **leaf node** is mamba-evicted, both its KV cache and mamba state are freed, and the node is deleted (since a leaf without mamba state is useless).\n\n### Insert with mamba state (lines 1111-1181, `_insert_helper`):\nOn insertion, if the key fully matches an existing node that has been tombstoned (`node.mamba_value is None`), the mamba value is restored (line 1169-1174). If the node already has a mamba value, the insertion reports `mamba_value_exist = True` (line 1176). For new nodes, both `value` and `mamba_value` are set.\n\n### Tombstone cleanup (lines 1183-1204, `_iteratively_delete_tombstone_leaf`):\nAfter evicting a leaf node, the system checks if the parent became a tombstone leaf (no children, no mamba value). If so, it iteratively cleans up the parent chain, freeing KV cache and removing from the full LRU list, up to the root or a locked node.\n\n---\n\n## 4. The only synchronization-like event is `draft_input.verify_done.synchronize()` at line 2179, which is for speculative decoding, not mamba.\n\n---\n\n### Summary of Mamba State Tracking Architecture\n\n1. **Per-request tracking:** Each `Req` holds its own mamba pool index, a 2-element ping-pong buffer, a flip index (0 or 1), the last tracked seqlen, and an optional branching seqlen from prefix cache matching.\n\n2. **Ping-pong double buffering:** Two state slots per request allow writing new state while the old state is still readable. The `mamba_next_track_idx` flips after each extend that triggers tracking (line 1844).\n\n3. **Conditional tracking:** Not every step saves mamba state. During extend, tracking only fires when `extend_input_len >= mamba_cache_chunk_size` (line 1807). During decode, tracking fires at periodic intervals controlled by `mamba_track_interval` (line 2170).\n\n4. **Branching support:** When a prefix cache match produces a `mamba_branching_seqlen`, the extend preparation may override the tracking seqlen to capture state at the branch point (lines 1849-1864), enabling copy-on-write mamba state sharing.\n\n5. **GPU sync concern:** The `.item()` call at line 1810 is called inside a per-request loop during extend preparation. If `mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer` resides on GPU, this forces a GPU-to-CPU synchronization per request. Per-Request Mamba State (class `Req`, lines 664-674)\n\nFive fields on each `Req` object track mamba state per request:\n\n```python\n665        self.req_pool_idx: Optional[int] = None\n666        self.mamba_pool_idx: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None  # shape (1)\n667        self.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None  # shape (2)\n668        self.mamba_next_track_idx: Optional[int] = None  # 0 or 1\n669        self.mamba_last_track_seqlen: Optional[int] = (\n670            None  # seq len of the last cached mamba state\n671        )\n672        # the branching point seqlen to track mamba state. If set, given by prefix match,\n673        # it will be the tracked seqlen in the ping pong buffer for the right prefill pass.\n674        self.mamba_branching_seqlen: Optional[int] = None\n```\n\n**Key design:**\n- `mamba_pool_idx` -- index into the mamba state pool for this request (shape `(1)` tensor).\n- `mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer` -- a 2-element tensor holding two pool indices used in a ping-pong double-buffering scheme.\n- `mamba_next_track_idx` -- alternates between 0 and 1 to select which of the two ping-pong buffer slots to write the next mamba state into.\n- `mamba_last_track_seqlen` -- records the sequence length at which the last mamba state was saved.\n- `mamba_branching_seqlen` -- set during prefix matching; indicates a branching point where a new mamba state diverges from a shared prefix.\n\n---\n\n### 3. Prefix Matching Sets `mamba_branching_seqlen` (lines 976-997)\n\nIn `Req.init_next_round_input()`:\n\n```python\n976        if tree_cache is not None:\n977            if cow_mamba is None:\n978                cow_mamba = tree_cache.supports_mamba()\n979            match_result = tree_cache.match_prefix(\n980                MatchPrefixParams(\n981                    key=RadixKey(token_ids=token_ids, extra_key=self.extra_key),\n982                    req=self,\n983                    cow_mamba=cow_mamba,\n984                )\n985            )\n986            (\n987                self.prefix_indices,\n988                self.last_node,\n989                self.last_host_node,\n990                self.host_hit_length,\n991                self.mamba_branching_seqlen,\n992            ) = (\n993                match_result.device_indices,\n994                match_result.last_device_node,\n995                match_result.last_host_node,\n996                match_result.host_hit_length,\n997                match_result.mamba_branching_seqlen,\n998            )\n```\n\nThe `cow_mamba` flag (copy-on-write for mamba) is passed to `match_prefix`, and `mamba_branching_seqlen` is returned from the result.\n\n---\n\n### 4. Reset on Retract (lines 1204-1208)\n\nWhen a request is retracted/recycled, all mamba fields are cleared:\n\n```python\n1204        self.mamba_pool_idx = None\n1205        self.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer = None\n1206        self.mamba_next_track_idx = None\n1207        self.mamba_last_track_seqlen = None\n1208        self.mamba_branching_seqlen = None\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. Per-Batch Mamba Fields on `ScheduleBatch` (lines 1344-1347)\n\n```python\n1344    # For hybrid GDN prefix cache\n1345    mamba_track_indices: torch.Tensor = None  # shape: [b], int64\n1346    mamba_track_mask: torch.Tensor = None  # shape: [b], bool\n1347    mamba_track_seqlens: torch.Tensor = None  # shape: [b], int64\n```\n\n- `mamba_track_indices` -- per-request index into the mamba state pool (which ping-pong slot to write to).\n- `mamba_track_mask` -- boolean mask indicating which requests should track/save mamba state in this step.\n- `mamba_track_seqlens` -- the sequence lengths at which to track mamba state.\n\n---\n\n### 6. Extend-Path Preparation (lines 1623-1686, 1762-1777)\n\nDuring `prepare_for_extend()`, CPU lists are built per request and then converted to GPU tensors:\n\n**Lines 1623-1625** -- Initialize CPU accumulation lists:\n```python\n1623        mamba_track_mask_cpu = []\n1624        mamba_track_indices_cpu = []\n1625        mamba_track_seqlens_cpu = []\n```\n\n**Lines 1680-1686** -- Per-request, conditionally call the mamba preparation helper:\n```python\n1680            if get_global_server_args().enable_mamba_extra_buffer():\n1681                self._mamba_radix_cache_v2_req_prepare_for_extend(\n1682                    req,\n1683                    mamba_track_mask_cpu,\n1684                    mamba_track_indices_cpu,\n1685                    mamba_track_seqlens_cpu,\n1686                )\n```\n\n**Lines 1762-1777** -- Convert CPU lists to GPU tensors:\n```python\n1762        if get_global_server_args().enable_mamba_extra_buffer():\n1763            self.mamba_track_indices = torch.tensor(\n1764                mamba_track_indices_cpu,\n1765                dtype=torch.int64,\n1766                device=self.device,\n1767            )\n1768            self.mamba_track_mask = torch.tensor(\n1769                mamba_track_mask_cpu,\n1770                dtype=torch.bool,\n1771                device=self.device,\n1772            )\n1773            self.mamba_track_seqlens = torch.tensor(\n1774                mamba_track_seqlens_cpu,\n1775                dtype=torch.int64,\n1776                device=self.device,\n1777            )\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7. Core Mamba Extend Logic: `_mamba_radix_cache_v2_req_prepare_for_extend` (lines 1788-1866)\n\nThis is the most complex mamba logic. Full method:\n\n**Lines 1788-1794** -- Signature:\n```python\n1788    def _mamba_radix_cache_v2_req_prepare_for_extend(\n1789        self,\n1790        req: Req,\n1791        mamba_track_mask_cpu: List[bool],\n1792        mamba_track_indices_cpu: List[int],\n1793        mamba_track_seqlens_cpu: List[int],\n1794    ):\n```\n\n**Lines 1795-1804** -- Helper to force retrieval from `h` (internal state buffer) rather than `last_recurrent_state`:\n```python\n1795        def _force_track_h(i: int) -> int:\n1796            assert i % FLA_CHUNK_SIZE == 0\n1797            # There are 3 cases for mamba_track_seqlen passed to mamba_track_seqlens_cpu:\n1798            # 1) aligned with FLA_CHUNK_SIZE-> retrieve from last_recurrent_state\n1799            #    a) is the last position -> retrieve from last_recurrent_state\n1800            #    b) is NOT the last position -> retrieve from h\n1801            # 2) unaligned with FLA_CHUNK_SIZE -> retrieve from h\n1802            # Currently, the math calculation only supports case 1a and 2. So for 1b, we need to add 1\n1803            # to force the math calculation to retrieve the correct mamba state from h.\n1804            return i + 1\n```\n\n**Lines 1806-1811** -- Mask computation and `.item()` call (GPU-to-CPU sync):\n```python\n1806        mamba_cache_chunk_size = get_global_server_args().mamba_cache_chunk_size\n1807        mask = req.extend_input_len >= mamba_cache_chunk_size\n1808        mamba_track_mask_cpu.append(mask)\n1809        mamba_track_indices_cpu.append(\n1810            req.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer[req.mamba_next_track_idx].item()\n1811        )\n```\n\n**THIS IS THE ONLY `.item()` CALL IN MAMBA CONTEXT** (line 1810). It extracts a scalar from the ping-pong buffer tensor. This is a potential GPU synchronization point if `mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer` is on GPU.\n\n**Lines 1812-1865** -- Compute which seqlen to track, handle alignment, flip the ping-pong index, handle branching:\n```python\n1812        mamba_track_seqlen = -1\n1813        if mask:\n1821            mamba_track_seqlen = len(req.prefix_indices) + req.extend_input_len\n1825            mamba_track_seqlen_aligned = (\n1826                len(req.prefix_indices)\n1827                + (req.extend_input_len // mamba_cache_chunk_size)\n1828                * mamba_cache_chunk_size\n1829            )\n1844            req.mamba_next_track_idx = (\n1845                self.req_to_token_pool.get_mamba_ping_pong_other_idx(\n1846                    req.mamba_next_track_idx\n1847                )\n1848            )\n1849            if req.mamba_branching_seqlen is not None:\n1863                    mamba_track_seqlen = _force_track_h(req.mamba_branching_seqlen)\n1864                    mamba_track_seqlen_aligned = req.mamba_branching_seqlen\n1865            req.mamba_last_track_seqlen = mamba_track_seqlen_aligned\n1866        mamba_track_seqlens_cpu.append(mamba_track_seqlen)\n```\n\nThe ping-pong index flip at line 1844 means the next time this request needs to track state, it will use the alternate buffer slot, implementing double buffering.\n\n---\n\n### 8. Decode-Path Preparation (lines 2145-2173)\n\nIn `prepare_for_decode()`, mamba tracking for decode steps is set up:\n\n```python\n2145        if get_global_server_args().enable_mamba_extra_buffer():\n2146            if len(self.reqs) == 0:\n2147                self.mamba_track_indices = torch.empty(\n2148                    (0,), dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device\n2149                )\n2150            else:\n2151                # already on device\n2152                all_buffers = torch.stack(\n2153                    [req.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer for req in self.reqs]\n2154                )\n2155                idx = (\n2156                    torch.tensor(\n2157                        [req.mamba_next_track_idx for req in self.reqs],\n2158                        dtype=torch.int64,\n2159                        pin_memory=True,\n2160                    )\n2161                    .unsqueeze(1)\n2162                    .to(device=all_buffers.device, non_blocking=True)\n2163                )\n2164                self.mamba_track_indices = (\n2165                    torch.gather(all_buffers, 1, idx).squeeze(1).to(torch.int64)\n2166                )\n2167\n2168            # async H2D\n2169            self.mamba_track_mask = (\n2170                (self.seq_lens_cpu % get_global_server_args().mamba_track_interval == 0)\n2171                .pin_memory()\n2172                .to(device=self.device, non_blocking=True)\n2173            )\n```\n\nIn the decode path, unlike extend, there is **no `.item()` call**. Instead, `torch.gather` is used to batch-select the correct ping-pong indices from all requests at once -- this is a more efficient GPU-side approach. The mask is computed on CPU and transferred asynchronously via pinned memory.\n\n---\n\n### 9. filter_batch and merge_batch Reset Mamba (lines 2236-2238, 2291-2293)\n\nBoth `filter_batch` and `merge_batch` null out all mamba batch tensors. They will be recomputed in the next `prepare_for_decode` or `prepare_for_extend` call:\n\n**filter_batch (line 2236):**\n```python\n2236        self.mamba_track_indices = None\n2237        self.mamba_track_mask = None\n2238        self.mamba_track_seqlens = None\n```\n\n**merge_batch (line 2291):**\n```python\n2291        self.mamba_track_indices = None\n2292        self.mamba_track_mask = None\n2293        self.mamba_track_seqlens = None\n```\n\n---\n\n### 10. Propagation to `ModelWorkerBatch` (lines 2390-2392, 2417-2419, 2576-2579)\n\nMamba fields flow from `ScheduleBatch` to `ModelWorkerBatch` via `get_model_worker_batch()` (lines 2390-2392) and via `copy()` (lines 2417-2419):\n\n```python\n2390            mamba_track_indices=self.mamba_track_indices,\n2391            mamba_track_mask=self.mamba_track_mask,\n2392            mamba_track_seqlens=self.mamba_track_seqlens,\n```\n\nAnd `ModelWorkerBatch` declares these as optional fields (lines 2576-2579):\n```python\n2576    # For mamba state tracking\n2577    mamba_track_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None  # shape: [b], int64\n2578    mamba_track_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None  # shape: [b], bool\n2579    mamba_track_seqlens: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None  # shape: [b], int64\n```\n\n---\n\n### 11. `.item()` Calls Summary\n\nThere are exactly **2** `.item()` calls in the file:\n\n| Line | Code | Context |\n|------|------|---------|\n| **1810** | `req.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer[req.mamba_next_track_idx].item()` | **Mamba-related.** Extracts the pool index from the ping-pong buffer tensor. Called once per request during extend preparation. This is a potential GPU sync point if the tensor is on GPU. |\n| **2231** | `self.seq_lens.sum().item()` | **Not mamba-related.** In `filter_batch()`, recomputes `seq_lens_sum`. This is a GPU sync point since `self.seq_lens` is on device. |\n\n---\n\n### 12. No `.cpu()`, `.tolist()`, or `torch.cuda.synchronize()` Calls\n\nThere are **zero** occurrences of `.cpu()`, `.tolist()`, or `torch.cuda.synchronize()` anywhere in the file in mamba-related or other contexts (within this file). The only synchronization-like event is `draft_input.verify_done.synchronize()` at line 2179, which is for speculative decoding, not mamba.\n\n---\n\n### Summary of Mamba State Tracking Architecture\n\n1. **Per-request tracking:** Each `Req` holds its own mamba pool index, a 2-element ping-pong buffer, a flip index (0 or 1), the last tracked seqlen, and an optional branching seqlen from prefix cache matching.\n\n2. **Ping-pong double buffering:** Two state slots per request allow writing new state while the old state is still readable. The `mamba_next_track_idx` flips after each extend that triggers tracking (line 1844).\n\n3. **Conditional tracking:** Not every step saves mamba state. During extend, tracking only fires when `extend_input_len >= mamba_cache_chunk_size` (line 1807). During decode, tracking fires at periodic intervals controlled by `mamba_track_interval` (line 2170).\n\n4. **Branching support:** When a prefix cache match produces a `mamba_branching_seqlen`, the extend preparation may override the tracking seqlen to capture state at the branch point (lines 1849-1864), enabling copy-on-write mamba state sharing.\n\n5. **GPU sync concern:** The `.item()` call at line 1810 is called inside a per-request loop during extend preparation. If `mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer` resides on GPU, this forces a GPU-to-CPU synchronization per request. Aligned case (len % FLA_CHUNK_SIZE == 0): In this case, FLA will store the to-cache\n            state in the last_recurrent_state.\n          - Unaligned case (len % FLA_CHUNK_SIZE != 0): The last_recurrent_state includes the\n            unaligned position, but we only want state up to the last chunk boundary.\n            We must extract from the intermediate `h` tensor at the appropriate chunk index.\n\n        We compute the src and dst indices for all requests that need to be cached\n        (i.e. mamba_track_mask is True) based on the rule above.\n\n        For example:\n        1. If chunked prefill length is < 64, then only final state has value. In this case we\n           cache `final` state.\n        2. if chunked prefill length == 64, then only final state has value. In this case we\n           cache pos 64, from `final` state\n        3. if chunked prefill length >64 and < 128, then both h and final state have value.\n           We cache pos 64 from `h` state\n        4. if chunked prefill length ==128, then both h and final state have value. We cache\n           pos 128 from `final` state. Note `h` doesn't include the pos 128.\n\n        Returns:\n            track_ssm_h_src: Source indices into the packed `h` tensor (for unaligned seqs)\n            track_ssm_h_dst: Destination cache slot indices (for unaligned seqs)\n            track_ssm_final_src: Source indices into last_recurrent_state buffer (for aligned seqs)\n            track_ssm_final_dst: Destination cache slot indices (for aligned seqs)\n        \"\"\"\n        # Move to CPU to avoid kernel launches for masking operations\n        mamba_track_mask = forward_batch.mamba_track_mask.cpu()\n        extend_seq_lens = forward_batch.extend_seq_lens.cpu()\n        mamba_track_indices = forward_batch.mamba_track_indices.cpu()\n        mamba_cache_indices = mamba_cache_indices.cpu()\n        mamba_track_seqlens = forward_batch.mamba_track_seqlens.cpu()\n        prefix_lens = forward_batch.extend_prefix_lens.cpu()\n\n        # Calculate the number of hidden states per request\n        num_h_states = (extend_seq_lens - 1) // FLA_CHUNK_SIZE + 1\n\n        # Calculate the starting offset for each sequence in the packed batch\n        track_ssm_src_offset = torch.zeros_like(num_h_states)\n        track_ssm_src_offset[1:] = torch.cumsum(num_h_states[:-1], dim=0)\n\n        # Filter variables by track mask\n        lens_to_track = mamba_track_seqlens - prefix_lens\n        lens_masked = lens_to_track[mamba_track_mask]\n        offset_masked = track_ssm_src_offset[mamba_track_mask]\n        dst_masked = mamba_track_indices[mamba_track_mask]\n\n        # Determine if the sequence ends at a chunk boundary\n        is_aligned = (lens_masked % FLA_CHUNK_SIZE) == 0\n","numLines":80,"startLine":258,"totalLines":995}}}

Mamba/Hybrid Model Forward Path and State Management\n\n**State pool**: `MambaPool` (in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/mem_cache/memory_pool.py`, lines 190-445) stores conv state (list of tensors) and temporal/SSM state (single tensor) per mamba layer. Pool indices are GPU tensors.\n\n**Request-level tracking**: Each `Req` carries `mamba_pool_idx` (primary state slot), `mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer` (2-element tensor with alternate slot indices), `mamba_next_track_idx` (0 or 1), and `mamba_last_track_seqlen`.\n\n**Forward flow**:\n1. Scheduler's `prepare_for_extend/decode` builds `mamba_track_indices`, `mamba_track_mask`, `mamba_track_seqlens` tensors\n2. These flow through `ModelWorkerBatch` -> `ForwardBatch` -> `MambaAttnBackendBase._forward_metadata()`\n3. During extend, `_init_track_conv_indices` and `_init_track_ssm_indices` compute where to save conv/SSM states\n4. Per-layer: `_track_mamba_state_extend` (extend) or `track_mamba_states_if_needed` Triton kernel (decode) copies states to the ping-pong buffer\n\n**Decode path (efficient)**: Uses `torch.gather` to batch-select ping-pong indices (line 2164-2165) and `track_mamba_states_if_needed` Triton kernel with on-device mask check -- no per-request `.item()`.\n\n**Extend path (has sync ops)**: Uses per-request `.item()` (line 1810) and per-batch `.cpu()` transfers (lines 315-320), plus per-layer `.any()` check (line 618).\n\n---\n\n### 4. How Prefix Cache Interacts with Mamba State Tracking\n\n**`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/mem_cache/mamba_radix_cache.py`**\n\nThe `MambaRadixCache` extends the radix tree with mamba state awareness:\n\n- **Dual values per node**: Each `TreeNode` has both `value` (KV cache indices) and `mamba_value` (mamba state pool indices). Mamba states can be evicted independently, creating \"tombstone\" nodes (`mamba_value is None` but `value` exists).\n\n- **Dual LRU lists**: Separate `full_lru_list` and `mamba_lru_list` for independent eviction policies.\n\n- **Prefix matching with mamba awareness** (lines 956-996): The match only extends to the deepest node with a non-None `mamba_value`. Tombstoned nodes are skipped. When a match hits tombstoned nodes deeper in the tree, `mamba_branching_seqlen` is set to indicate where mamba state re-computation is needed.\n\n- **Copy-on-write mamba states** (lines 1049-1066): During prefix matching with `cow_mamba=True`, the matched node's mamba state is copied into the request's private mamba pool via `mamba_pool.fork_from()`.\n\n- **Cache insertion** (lines 1111-1181): When caching a finished/unfinished request, both KV cache and mamba state are stored. With `extra_buffer`, the mamba state comes from `req.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer` (the ping-pong slot that holds the committed state). Without `extra_buffer`, it comes from `req.mamba_pool_idx` directly.\n\n- **No GPU sync in this file**: The `mamba_radix_cache.py` file itself contains zero `.item()`, `.cpu()`, `.tolist()`, or `torch.cuda.synchronize()` calls. All operations use GPU tensors or tensor metadata (`.shape`).\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table: Hot-Path Sync Operations\n\n| # | File | Line | Operation | Frequency | extra_buffer only? |\n|---|------|------|-----------|-----------|-------------------|\n| 1 | `schedule_batch.py` | 1810 | `.item()` on GPU tensor | Per-request in extend loop | Yes |\n| 2 | `hybrid_linear_attn_backend.py` | 315-320 | 6x `.cpu()` on GPU tensors | Once per extend batch | Yes |\n| 3 | `hybrid_linear_attn_backend.py` | 618 | `.any()` on GPU tensor | Per-layer per extend batch | Yes |\n| 4 | `mamba2_metadata.py` | 201 | `torch.any().item()` | Once per extend/mixed batch | No (all mamba models) |\n| 5 | `causal_conv1d.py` | 27 | `.cpu().tolist()` | Fallback only (normally avoided) | No |\n| 6 | `schedule_batch.py` | 2231 | `.sum().item()` | Per filter_batch | No |\n\nThe **highest-impact sync point** is #1 (line 1810 in schedule_batch.py) because it runs inside a per-request Python loop during extend preparation, causing a GPU pipeline stall for every request. Mamba/Hybrid Model Forward Path and State Management\n\n**State pool**: `MambaPool` (in `/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/mem_cache/memory_pool.py`, lines 190-445) stores conv state (list of tensors) and temporal/SSM state (single tensor) per mamba layer. Pool indices are GPU tensors.\n\n**Request-level tracking**: Each `Req` carries `mamba_pool_idx` (primary state slot), `mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer` (2-element tensor with alternate slot indices), `mamba_next_track_idx` (0 or 1), and `mamba_last_track_seqlen`.\n\n**Forward flow**:\n1. Scheduler's `prepare_for_extend/decode` builds `mamba_track_indices`, `mamba_track_mask`, `mamba_track_seqlens` tensors\n2. These flow through `ModelWorkerBatch` -> `ForwardBatch` -> `MambaAttnBackendBase._forward_metadata()`\n3. During extend, `_init_track_conv_indices` and `_init_track_ssm_indices` compute where to save conv/SSM states\n4. Per-layer: `_track_mamba_state_extend` (extend) or `track_mamba_states_if_needed` Triton kernel (decode) copies states to the ping-pong buffer\n\n**Decode path (efficient)**: Uses `torch.gather` to batch-select ping-pong indices (line 2164-2165) and `track_mamba_states_if_needed` Triton kernel with on-device mask check -- no per-request `.item()`.\n\n**Extend path (has sync ops)**: Uses per-request `.item()` (line 1810) and per-batch `.cpu()` transfers (lines 315-320), plus per-layer `.any()` check (line 618).\n\n---\n\n### 4. How Prefix Cache Interacts with Mamba State Tracking\n\n**`/sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/mem_cache/mamba_radix_cache.py`**\n\nThe `MambaRadixCache` extends the radix tree with mamba state awareness:\n\n- **Dual values per node**: Each `TreeNode` has both `value` (KV cache indices) and `mamba_value` (mamba state pool indices). Mamba states can be evicted independently, creating \"tombstone\" nodes (`mamba_value is None` but `value` exists).\n\n- **Dual LRU lists**: Separate `full_lru_list` and `mamba_lru_list` for independent eviction policies.\n\n- **Prefix matching with mamba awareness** (lines 956-996): The match only extends to the deepest node with a non-None `mamba_value`. Tombstoned nodes are skipped. When a match hits tombstoned nodes deeper in the tree, `mamba_branching_seqlen` is set to indicate where mamba state re-computation is needed.\n\n- **Copy-on-write mamba states** (lines 1049-1066): During prefix matching with `cow_mamba=True`, the matched node's mamba state is copied into the request's private mamba pool via `mamba_pool.fork_from()`.\n\n- **Cache insertion** (lines 1111-1181): When caching a finished/unfinished request, both KV cache and mamba state are stored. With `extra_buffer`, the mamba state comes from `req.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer` (the ping-pong slot that holds the committed state). Without `extra_buffer`, it comes from `req.mamba_pool_idx` directly.\n\n- **No GPU sync in this file**: The `mamba_radix_cache.py` file itself contains zero `.item()`, `.cpu()`, `.tolist()`, or `torch.cuda.synchronize()` calls. All operations use GPU tensors or tensor metadata (`.shape`).\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table: Hot-Path Sync Operations\n\n| # | File | Line | Operation | Frequency | extra_buffer only? |\n|---|------|------|-----------|-----------|-------------------|\n| 1 | `schedule_batch.py` | 1810 | `.item()` on GPU tensor | Per-request in extend loop | Yes |\n| 2 | `hybrid_linear_attn_backend.py` | 315-320 | 6x `.cpu()` on GPU tensors | Once per extend batch | Yes |\n| 3 | `hybrid_linear_attn_backend.py` | 618 | `.any()` on GPU tensor | Per-layer per extend batch | Yes |\n| 4 | `mamba2_metadata.py` | 201 | `torch.any().item()` | Once per extend/mixed batch | No (all mamba models) |\n| 5 | `causal_conv1d.py` | 27 | `.cpu().tolist()` | Fallback only (normally avoided) | No |\n| 6 | `schedule_batch.py` | 2231 | `.sum().item()` | Per filter_batch | No |\n\nThe **highest-impact sync point** is #1 (line 1810 in schedule_batch.py) because it runs inside a per-request Python loop during extend preparation, causing a GPU pipeline stall for every request.
